metadata.title: Generation Probabilities are Not Enough: Improving Error Highlighting in AI Code Suggestions

figures.description: Microsoft Research and Stanford University Logo

content: Programmers increasingly use AI generated code (e.g., from CoPilot) in IDEs!
However, generated code may be erroneous!We need to help programmers double-check AI code and identify when and where it may be incorrect.
We explore two options to highlight uncertain, potentially erroneous regions:
Usegeneration probabilitiesor "confidence scores" to show where the model is uncertain
Learn anedit modelto show where programmers are most likely to make edits
Mixed-methods, within-subjects study with 30 programmers showed thatedit model leads to:
compared to the generation probability and no-highlights

figures.description: Bar chart showing task completion time: Prediction, Generation Probability, and Edit Model
Bar chart showing survival of tokens for highlighted vs non-highlighted code
Bar chart showing user preference for Edit Model over Generation Probability
